chats.text: so um what should I tell them for tomorrow then like do i just say I need more time to think about it or...
{NAME} if you tell him to take a day what does that even mean? Do I have to sit in the breakroom and stare at a wall?
{NAME} how do i tell them i'm not ready to work intense 12-hour days under constant stress without sounding like a quitter?
{NAME} what if i just say i'm aligned with the work but need a day to decompress from the responsibility?
okay i think i understand the point about alignment now thank you that helps
Did anyone else just get assigned 'stakeholder alignment' as a weekly task? I feel like it's a new role.
{NAME}, I'm telling you. That last meeting was the sound of a startup coffin closing softly. I heard it in my bones.
{NAME}, what if I tell you that 'aligned with our product' is now a euphemism for 'we need someone else to suffer'?
The 'infrastructure' they're talking about. Is it still the same infrastructure from 2015 or did we rebuild on a foundation of existential dread?
wait he just said i can have the same job as everyone else right?
hey bro my new title is 'Full-Time Intern' and it's like a full-time internship program. I'm learning while working.
does anyone else feel like we're in a corporate fantasy? because my intern hours are 50+ per week
they gave me the internship but no raise. so basically I'm paying them to train me on how to be an employee.
i walked into today's standup with a coffee and it was still 4 minutes late because of the 'strategic alignment discussion'
my mentor told me to take extra hours this week to prove my dedication. i worked through lunch and they thanked me for showing initiative.
our KPI is now 'reducing our reliance on external consultants' which is a full-time job but apparently just more of the same
i saw someone else's name in the promotion list today and i had to remind myself that 'legacy' means something different here.
does anyone know what time the actual workday ends or is it just until the stakeholders stop breathing
my manager says my performance review is 'scheduled for next quarter' which is like 3 months away but also forever.
i submitted a change request form that took me 4 days to complete and got approved instantly. why is my approval so hard?
they're hiring external contractors for the same thing i'm doing but at a rate that makes sense. i have no words.
the 'vision' meeting is every Tuesday and it's always just more meetings about meetings
i'm reading a book on corporate wellness but it feels like it was written by someone who's never left the building.
someone just asked if i had any 'bandwidth' and i don't know how to answer that anymore
my internship certificate expires on a date that's also the next fiscal year end so there will be no certification.
the exit survey form is 12 pages long and asks 'what would you change' which is just asking what i'm doing wrong
Okay, so I get it. It's intense here. And I'm grateful for everything... but also, I don't know if I'm built for this. Like, literally. Are you?
I saw that offer letter on your desk like three times today and kept thinking about it. You're my friend right? Tell me what's real.
Does anyone else just feel a lot of pressure in meetings? Like everyone has to agree with you even if they don't?
My mom asked me last night what I do for fun. And I said 'LARP.' She asked if that was the board game where we dress up like superheroes.
I'm so happy to be here and feel like this is my purpose, but also — can someone tell me how much more money people make at 30?
He told us yesterday that the early days were 'like hell.' I'm trying to remember if there was a break room where we could sit on chairs.
I know everyone wants what's best for the company and all, but sometimes when you look around at how much time is spent in meetings... it feels like we're not even doing any real work.

What if I told you that last night I dreamed about a startup? It had no playbook. No infrastructure. Just us, really figuring things out together. We almost lost everything like five times in the dream and survived. You were there.
I was made aware of how much my peers are doing outside these walls every single day and it's... it's just so different from what we're doing here.
If I stay here, you know my life. It's comfortable. It's safe. But if I go that way? That's a wild thing to ask yourself about, isn't it?
